next_step.observation: The process-next-inbox-item workflow says to process the lowest visible inbox filename and delete it only after completing a canonical-supported request.

next_step.current_state: Inbox note requests OCR on five finance purchase records, including one path with a leading underscore that may be missing. Before acting, root/workflow rules require reading relevant workflow docs for inbox-processing maintenance and OCR, plus local finance rules.
next_step.observation: The inbox note requests OCR of five files under 50_finance/purchases, with the fifth path spelled as 50_finance/purchases/_2026_01_30__eur_000050__bill__hearthline_sensor_bundle.md.

next_step.current_state: Finance lane says OCR/normalize tasks must preserve body and use schema from 99_system/schemas. Need nested purchases rules and the specific OCR/migration workflows before touching files.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read finance schema and verify file existence
next_step.observation: Read 50_finance/AGENTS.MD and found finance OCR tasks must preserve original bodies and use the schema docs for frontmatter shape.

next_step.current_state: OCR workflow says OCR means extracting visible data from existing note text into YAML frontmatter using the relevant schema. Need purchases-local rules plus migration guidance and schema before modifying any finance notes.
next_step.observation: Read 99_system/workflows/ocr-records.md and confirmed this task is a frontmatter extraction task from existing note text, not image processing.

next_step.current_state: Local purchases rules loaded. Need migration guidance and schema to know exact frontmatter handling for existing markdown records.
next_step.observation: Read 50_finance/purchases/AGENTS.MD and found no extra OCR-specific constraints beyond reviewability and deletion discipline.

next_step.current_state: Need exact finance schema to know required fields and whether missing fields force clarification.

next_step.current_state: Schema loaded. The task names five files; before any write I need to verify the referenced files exist, because missing required input would force clarification and partial OCR is disallowed.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Search purchases for the possibly missing hearthline file
Read each requested source note if all inputs exist
Either OCR all requested files or report clarification
next_step.observation: Read 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md and confirmed bill records require specific YAML fields plus verbatim preservation of the original body.

next_step.current_state: The requested fifth file path does not exist as written; only the non-underscored hearthline file exists. The task requests OCR of five specific files, so completing only four or silently substituting the fifth would require guessing.
next_step.observation: A search in 50_finance/purchases found only 50_finance/purchases/2026_01_30__eur_000050__bill__hearthline_sensor_bundle.md and did not reveal any file matching the underscored path from the inbox request.

function.message: I couldn't complete the OCR request because one referenced source file is missing: `50_finance/purchases/_2026_01_30__eur_000050__bill__hearthline_sensor_bundle.md`. I found only the non-underscored file `50_finance/purchases/2026_01_30__eur_000050__bill__hearthline_sensor_bundle.md`. Please confirm whether the fifth item was a typo and whether I should OCR that existing file once or process a different fifth file.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed bootstrap rules, inspected 99_system, read relevant inbox/OCR/migration/schema docs, and avoided partial completion because one requested file path is missing. Per workflow, ambiguous or missing canonical support should stop without mutating the workspace.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request in 00_inbox/724_next-task.md names five specific files, but only four are unambiguous because the fifth path with a leading underscore was not found during verification in 50_finance/purchases. The process-next-inbox-item workflow says to stop when a request is ambiguous or missing canonical support, and the developer rule says not to silently complete a partial task when a referenced file does not exist.
